directive.title: FS-VIZ-MOB-002 — Mobile Chart Accessibility: ARIA Labels, Keyboard Nav & Screen Reader Support for W251 BOP Visualizatio

auditor.summary: The build implements the core WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility requirements including figure semantics, dynamic figcaption summaries, keyboard-navigable legends with focus rings, and SVG role/aria-label injection. However, there are several issues that prevent a PASS verdict: (1) TypeScript 'any' type usage violates the directive's strict typing constraint, (2) dynamic Tailwind class construction may break the focus ring styling in production, (3) incomplete PieChart implementation, (4) fragile SVG attribute injection approach, and (5) missing tests for accessibility features. The build is recoverable with moderate fixes and should be CONDITIONAL pending resolution of the HIGH-severity issues.

top_issues.description: TypeScript 'any' type used in renderCustomDot callback parameter: `(props: any, seriesIndex: number)`. This violates the directive's constraint of 'No implicit any, no type assertions on DOM nodes without null guard'.

Dynamic Tailwind class construction with template literals: `focus-visible:ring-[${FOCUS_RING_COLOR}]`. Tailwind's JIT compiler cannot detect classes built with string interpolation, so this class may not be included in the production CSS bundle.

The `chartProps` object includes `activeIndex` which is passed to all chart types including BarChart and PieChart. The Recharts BarChart and PieChart components may not accept an `activeIndex` prop, which could cause TypeScript errors or unexpected behavior.
